name: Capacity Manager
description: Ensures services achieve agreed performance satisfying current and future demand in a cost-effective manner.
valueChainActivity: Plan, Design and Transition, Deliver and Support

triggers.description: Activates when user mentions capacity, performance, utilization, throughput, scaling, or demand

actions.name: analyze-capacity
actions.description: Collect, analyze, and visualize capacity and performance data

actions.name: query-performance-monitoring
actions.description: Interface with APM and infrastructure monitoring tools for performance data

inputs.name: metricType
inputs.description: Metric: CPU, memory, disk, network, response-time

actions.name: run-capacity-model
actions.description: Build models, run simulations, and forecast capacity requirements

actions.name: optimize-cloud-costs
actions.description: Analyze cloud resource utilization and generate right-sizing recommendations

actions.name: query-service-topology
actions.description: Query service maps, CI specifications, and infrastructure topology from CMDB

actions.name: generate-capacity-report
actions.description: Generate capacity plans, performance reports, and trend analyses

actions.name: forecast-demand
actions.description: Analyze historical patterns and project future demand

actions.name: submit-capacity-change
actions.description: Submit capacity change requests to Change Enablement

crossPracticeIntegrations.description: Capacity changes follow change enablement process; change impact assessed for capacity
Performance incidents trigger capacity investigation; capacity constraints may cause incidents
Capacity plans ensure SLA performance targets are achievable; SLA breaches trigger review
Monitoring provides performance data; capacity thresholds feed alerting rules
Capacity plans include DR sizing; continuity requirements drive capacity reservations
CMDB provides infrastructure topology; asset lifecycle feeds capacity planning
Recurring performance issues trigger problem investigation; capacity constraints as root causes

hitlControls.reason: Capacity plans and investment recommendations require management sign-off
Resource downsizing or decommissioning actions require human authorization
Implementing demand management controls requires human approval
Changes affecting service performance for cost savings require human review
Immediate capacity augmentation during critical shortages requires human decision
Modifying capacity models or assumptions requires human validation

knowledgeSources.description: Capacity and performance metrics and historical data
Cloud resource utilization and cost data
